summary: Iteration 2 — Frontend-only visual verification of two bug fixes: (1) Landing page bottom CTA readability, and (2) Gemini 3 Flash → Gemma 4 rename across all pages. CTA readability bug is FIXED. Gemma 4 rename is 95% complete — 6 of 7 pages have zero 'Gemini' occurrences, but the /architecture page still displays 'Gemini' 5 times (1 descriptive text + 2 code-snippet cards each containing 'gemini' twice).

ui_bugs.issue: Visible descriptive text still says 'Base64 image → Gemini vision → strict JSON extraction (blood group, units, hospital, urgency, doctor).' — must be renamed. Per the review requirement, no visible UI text should read 'Gemini Vision'. Fix: change 'Gemini vision' to 'Gemma 4 vision' in LAYERS[2].detail.

ui_bugs.component: Architecture.jsx — CodeCard bodies (CODE_HINT line ~17 and CODE_DOC line ~31)
ui_bugs.issue: Both code-snippet cards ('Multilingual Chat · Gemma 4' and 'Medical Document Vision') visibly render the line `.with_model("gemini", "gemini-3-flash-preview")`. The card titles say Gemma 4 but the code body exposes the real Gemini 3 Flash model ID to the user. Requirement said zero occurrences of 'Gemini' in DOM. Recommendation: replace the string in the on-screen code snippet with `.with_model("gemma", "gemma-4-preview")` (or similar hackathon-branded alias) while keeping the actual backend call untouched. This is a judgment call for the main agent since it means the displayed code no longer matches the real code — but that is consistent with the hackathon branding intent already applied elsewhere.
ui_bugs.selector: Architecture page → two <CodeCard> blocks near the top

verified_fixes: Landing page CTA section (right above the footer) now uses light bg-secondary background with a very faint (~0.08 opacity) red pulse decorative texture. Headline 'Mere father ke liye Kolkata mein urgent B+ blood chahiye.' is clearly readable in dark text-primary. 'EMERGENCY AI READY' overline, subtext, and red 'Start Emergency Request' button all have strong contrast and are fully visible — verified via screenshot.
Landing hero card correctly shows 'Gemma 4 · Scored 15 donors in 340 ms' (0 Gemini, 2 Gemma 4).
Layout footer shows 'Gemma 4 · Multilingual + Vision' on every page.
Dashboard shows 'Auto-generated by Gemma 4' on the AI outreach card.
Emergency page step 1 shows 'Gemma 4 parses patient, group, hospital, urgency'.
Document Analyzer page description and upload button both say 'Gemma 4' / 'Analyzing with Gemma 4 Vision…'.
Architecture page has 'Gemma 4 Core' card, 'How Gemma 4 is used' overline, and 'Multilingual Chat · Gemma 4' code card title all correctly rendering.

Regression smoke test: /, /emergency, /dashboard, /donors/register, /camps, /document, /architecture all load without console errors. Emergency page has chat input, Dashboard renders AI copy, Document page renders description — no visible breakage from the text/CSS refactor.

grep_verification./architecture: gemini=5 (1 in Document Vision detail + 2 per each of 2 CodeCard bodies), gemma4=5

action_items: In /app/frontend/src/pages/Architecture.jsx line 6: change 'Base64 image → Gemini vision → strict JSON extraction' to 'Base64 image → Gemma 4 vision → strict JSON extraction' (LAYERS[2].detail).

Optional but recommended for full brand compliance: in /app/frontend/src/pages/Architecture.jsx, update the two CODE_HINT / CODE_DOC template strings so the visible `.with_model("gemini", "gemini-3-flash-preview")` line is rebranded (backend Python code remains unchanged). If keeping real API string for engineering transparency is intentional, add a small note in the report — otherwise rename in the displayed snippet to match the Gemma 4 branding elsewhere.
critical_code_review_comments: Architecture.jsx is the only inconsistency remaining after the Gemma 4 branding sweep — worth one final pass to keep the brand story clean.
The 'red pulse' decorative image URL on Landing.jsx CTA is served from Unsplash on every render — consider hosting locally / lazy-loading for offline demo resilience (minor / optional).
No other regressions observed. Layout footer, Landing hero, Dashboard, Emergency, DocumentAnalyzer text changes render correctly.

success_rate.frontend: ~95% (2 outstanding UI text issues on Architecture page)

context_for_next_testing_agent: CTA readability is confirmed fixed. Only remaining task after main agent fix: re-grep /architecture for 'gemini' and expect 0 (or a documented, intentional carve-out for the code snippet). Backend was already 100% green in iteration 1 — no need to re-run backend_test.py unless E1 touches backend code.
rca of the issue: The rename was applied comprehensively across 6 of 7 pages, but Architecture.jsx retained one plaintext 'Gemini vision' in the LAYERS constant (line 6) and two occurrences of the real model string inside two on-screen code snippets (CODE_HINT and CODE_DOC constants). The card titles were updated to 'Gemma 4' but the code bodies displayed to the user were not synchronized. Landing CTA fix was applied correctly: switched from a dark red image background to bg-secondary with a 0.08-opacity decorative image and text-primary/text-secondary Tailwind tokens — contrast is now well above WCAG AA.
